title: Best Free VPN Services 2026: An Honest Look at What You Actually Get
description: Free VPNs come with real limitations: data caps, speed throttling, and in some cases questionable logging practices. Here is an honest breakdown of the best free options and what they cost you beyond money.
content:
The Real Cost of Free VPNs

Free VPN services exist because the provider makes money another way. The best free tiers use a freemium model: limit bandwidth or server access to push users toward a paid plan. The worst free services monetize user data. Knowing which model a provider uses is more important than the price.

Proton VPN Free Tier

Proton VPN offers a genuinely unlimited free tier with no data cap, but limits users to three server locations (Netherlands, Japan, US) and one simultaneous connection. Speed is throttled during peak hours. For light browsing and occasional privacy needs, it is the most reputable free option available. Proton is a Swiss non-profit, audited, open-source clients.

Windscribe Free

Windscribe gives 10 GB per month for free, expanding to 15 GB if you tweet about the service. Server selection includes about 10 countries. The free tier is honest about its limits and does not collect logs. Good for travel use or occasional needs.

What Free VPNs Cannot Do

Free tiers consistently fail at streaming geo-unblocking (servers get flagged quickly), P2P/torrenting (most block it on free tiers), and consistent speeds during peak hours. If those are your use cases, a paid VPN is a better investment.
